acceptance_targets: [chapter_05_dialogue_01] Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:159 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:215 still convey that Harry sees Carmen as already compromised, but neither line uses the phrase "You already decided" or the structure "or you'd have called the police"; fail if the pivot still reads like a copied calibration line.

[chapter_05_craft_01] Pass if the revised ending around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:213 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:225 still leaves Carmen unmistakably compromised, but the coda adds one new consequence or decision not already explicit in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:159 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:172 and does not rely on abstract labels such as "negotiate," "deadline," or "door" to explain the turn; fail if the coda mainly rephrases what the TV-room scene already proved.

[PROSE_AUTOCOMM_CH05] PASS if: (a) the five cited constructions are eliminated or replaced with non-formulaic alternatives; (b) the parking-lot reflection (lines 1001-1009) is compressed by at least 30%, with the insight about negotiating arriving through action or interior fragment rather than narrator announcement; (c) no more than 2 instances of any single auto-commentary formula remain in the full chapter. FAIL if the constructions persist or the narrator still explains what the dialogue already demonstrated.

[CALIBRATION_LEAKAGE_CH05] PASS if: (a) the line at 953 shares no more than 4 consecutive words with the calibration example 'You already decided, or you'd have called the police'; (b) the recall at line 1009 matches the revised line at 953; (c) the new line preserves Harry's leverage move — making Carmen acknowledge her complicity through her own inaction — without sounding like a different character. FAIL if 5+ consecutive words from the calibration line survive at either location.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:159 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:215 still convey that Harry sees Carmen as already compromised, but neither line uses the phrase "You already decided" or the structure "or you'd have called the police"; fail if the pivot still reads like a copied calibration line.
findings.problem: Harry's decisive leverage line near-verbatim echoes a calibration-only style-bible example ("You already decided, or you'd have called the police"), then gets repeated in Carmen's interior recall. Because this lands at the chapter's hinge, the reuse reads as voice-template leakage rather than a freshly authored turn, damaging voice integrity on first read.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:159 and the callback at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:215 with new wording grounded in the specific evidence of this scene: Carmen came to the TV room, shut the doors, sat down, and bargained for terms. Keep Harry's leverage shift, but make him diagnose her through those observed actions instead of reusing the calibration phrasing.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:213 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:225 still leaves Carmen unmistakably compromised, but the coda adds one new consequence or decision not already explicit in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:159 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:172 and does not rely on abstract labels such as "negotiate," "deadline," or "door" to explain the turn; fail if the coda mainly rephrases what the TV-room scene already proved.
findings.problem: The parking-lot coda overstates the chapter after the TV-room scene has already landed its turn. By the time Harry corners Carmen into "One week," the chapter has made her compromise legible. The later run of abstract diagnosis - "She had come to negotiate" and "the week wasn't a deadline. It was a door" - translates the same realization into cleaner thematic language instead of buying a new proof, cost, or decision. That is overstatement after arrival, and it softens the ending rather than sharpening it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or replace the interpretive material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:213 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_05.md:221. Preserve the state shift, but spend the parking-lot space on a consequence only this aftermath can provide: an aborted hotline call, a lie she now has to prepare for home, or another concrete action/image that shows the compromise without paraphrasing it.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if: (a) the five cited constructions are eliminated or replaced with non-formulaic alternatives; (b) the parking-lot reflection (lines 1001-1009) is compressed by at least 30%, with the insight about negotiating arriving through action or interior fragment rather than narrator announcement; (c) no more than 2 instances of any single auto-commentary formula remain in the full chapter. FAIL if the constructions persist or the narrator still explains what the dialogue already demonstrated.

evidence.excerpt: 829: His voice was different. Not just coherent — she'd heard him coherent, the tramadol correction, the hallway at 2:40 AM — but running on a completely different frequency. The confused Harry spoke in fragments, rising intonations, questions that dissolved before they finished. This Harry's voice was flat and level. Each sentence ended like a period looked: hard, complete, closed.

855: Carmen sat. She didn't decide to — her legs made the decision, or the heat made the decision, the TV room's heavy warmth pressing on her like a hand. The chair exhaled under her weight. Old vinyl, cracked along the arms, the cushion held together with facility-issue tape that had gone amber and stiff. She could feel the split upholstery through her scrubs.

983: Carmen pulled the door open. The hallway's half-light poured in, fluorescent and thin, and for a second the TV room's green glow looked like something she was stepping out of — not a room but a condition, a place with different rules. She walked out without looking back.

1003: Carmen pressed her forehead against the steering wheel. The vinyl was warm and slightly gritty — the accumulated film of summer, pollen and road dust and whatever came off her hands after twelve-hour shifts. She thought about the forty-seven pills. She thought about her license number on every count sheet, her signature, the careful documentation she'd been trained to maintain because documentation was protection, because if you charted it you could point to it and say *I did my job, I followed the protocol*. Except now the documentation was a trail. The trail led to her. The protection had become the map.

1007: She had come to negotiate. The word arrived in her mind fully formed, unambiguous, and she couldn't push it back. She had walked into that room with terms she hadn't articulated even to herself — conditions, limits, a framework for some arrangement she couldn't have described if someone had asked her. She hadn't come to report. She hadn't come to threaten. She'd come to negotiate, and Harry had seen it before she had, because Harry counted things, and what he was counting tonight was her.

findings.problem: Full-book pattern (all 18 chapters). This chapter is the Harry-Carmen confrontation — the novel's pivot. The prose auto-commentary undermines the scene's tension by over-explaining every gesture and moment. Specific instances: 'Each sentence ended like a period looked: hard, complete, closed' (line 829 — formula simile + 'closed/door' variant appearing across 4 chapters); 'She didn't decide to — her legs made the decision, or the heat made the decision' (line 855 — narrator explaining the character's involuntary action through a correction chain); 'not a room but a condition, a place with different rules' (line 983 — 'not X but Y' with appended explanation); 'the accumulated film of summer, pollen and road dust and whatever came off her hands' (line 1003 — catalogue explanation of sensory detail); 'She had come to negotiate. The word arrived in her mind fully formed, unambiguous' (line 1007 — narrator announcing and then explaining the insight). The chapter's dramatic power comes from Harry's reveal and Carmen's decision. The auto-commentary steps on both by translating the scene's pressure into thesis statements.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE — this chapter should retain 0 instances of these constructions (retention candidates are in chapters 1 and 15). Target lines: (1) Line 829: cut the simile chain 'like a period looked: hard, complete, closed.' Harry's voice IS flat and level — that's enough. The narrator doesn't need to explain what flat and level sounds like. (2) Line 855: cut the narrator's correction of Carmen's sitting. She sat. The vinyl exhaled. That's the scene. (3) Line 983: cut 'not a room but a condition.' The green glow image works without the thesis statement. (4) Lines 1003-1009: the parking-lot reflection scene runs long with narrator-delivered insight about what Carmen 'had come to do.' Compress: let the reader understand through Carmen's actions and silence, not through the narrator spelling out that she came to negotiate. (5) Throughout the chapter, reduce the narrator's explanatory bridges between Harry's lines and Carmen's reactions. The dialogue is strong — let it carry the scene without narrative translation.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if: (a) the line at 953 shares no more than 4 consecutive words with the calibration example 'You already decided, or you'd have called the police'; (b) the recall at line 1009 matches the revised line at 953; (c) the new line preserves Harry's leverage move — making Carmen acknowledge her complicity through her own inaction — without sounding like a different character. FAIL if 5+ consecutive words from the calibration line survive at either location.

evidence.excerpt: 953: "You already decided, or you'd have called the police instead of coming to talk to me."
1009: *You already decided, or you'd have called the police.*

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 953: "You already decided, or you'd have called the police instead of coming to talk to me."

findings.problem: Style-bible example line leakage. Harry's calibration line 'You already decided, or you'd have called the police' (marked CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY OR ECHO in the style bible) appears near-verbatim at line 953 ('You already decided, or you'd have called the police instead of coming to talk to me') and verbatim at line 1009 as Carmen's internal recall ('You already decided, or you'd have called the police'). The core 12-word phrase is reproduced intact. While the line reads naturally in context and a reader without the style bible wouldn't notice, the calibration contract was violated. This is a pivotal confrontation scene — the line is chapter-defining.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Harry's confrontation line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:953 to preserve Harry's register (short, declarative, leveraging Carmen's inaction) while ensuring no more than 4 consecutive words match the calibration text. Options: (a) Harry could make the same observation through his accounting frame — 'You've had three days and the phone didn't ring. That's not indecision, that's a down payment'; (b) Harry could use his silence-as-leverage style — state the fact of her presence and let the implication do the work. Then update line 1009 to match whatever the new line is, since it's Carmen recalling his words. The new line must feel like Harry's real register: clipped, final, calculated.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
